name: Thiocolchicoside
description: Thiocolchicoside is a muscle relaxant with anti-inflammatory and analgesic effects. It acts as a competitive GABAA receptor antagonist and also glycine receptor antagonist with similar potency and nicotinic acetylcholine receptors to a much lesser extent. It has powerful convulsant activity and should not be used in seizure-prone individuals.
indication: For the treatment of muscle spasms.
toxicity: Has been shown to cause chromosomal aneuploidy and male infertility. Should be avoided during all stages of pregnancy, lactation and puberty. Is a potential risk factor for cancer.
pharmacodynamics: Thiocholchicoside is a muscle relaxant that works through selective binding to the GABA receptor. It prevents muscle contractions by activating the GABAergic inhibitory motor pathway.
